host: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Laptop GPU (Blackwell sm_120 via compute_90 PTX JIT, nvcc 12.0)
method: Parity verification via make cuda-verify-fused after each change (the fused kernel is not yet wired into a GGML graph op — only the cuda_verify.cu harness's own scalar reference kernel is, so a graph-level llama-bench delta cannot isolate it; the optimizations are arithmetic-reduction / memory-traffic reductions whose magnitude is analytical and whose correctness is the fixture parity).

optimizations.name: shared-memory V-decode (decode each TBQ3 V block once per KV step instead of 4x per lane)
optimizations.before: every one of the 32 warp lanes re-ran all 4 block decodes per KV step (4 x {32 codebook lookups + Hadamard-32 butterfly + 32 sign multiplies}) and kept only its own element acc[c] = dec[lane]; ~31/32 of the decode arithmetic was wasted per block, x4 blocks, x n_kv steps.
optimizations.after: lanes 0..3 each decode one block into __shared__ float sh_dec[4][32]; __syncwarp(); all 32 lanes read sh_dec[c][lane]. V-decode arithmetic per KV step drops from 32*4 block-decodes to 4 (8x fewer than the 8-lane-subgroup variant the prior review proposed; the 32-element Hadamard is inherently sequential so a single thread per block is fine). 512 B shared mem / warp.
optimizations.expectedSpeedup: ~8x fewer V-decode FLOPs per KV step (32 block-decodes -> 4); the kernel is V-decode dominated at long context, so end-to-end this is roughly proportional minus the __syncwarp + shared-load overhead.
optimizations.parity: make cuda-verify-fused 1920/1920 outputs PASS, max diff 3.278e-7 (identical to pre-opt: the arithmetic and online-softmax rescale order are unchanged, only which lane performs each scalar op moved).

optimizations.name: register-hoist the 256-element Q sketch row
optimizations.before: qh[lane*8 + b] (8 fp32 per lane) was reloaded from global memory inside the inner t (KV-step) loop on every step.
optimizations.after: float qreg[8] loaded once at kernel entry, reused across all n_kv steps. Q is loop-invariant, so n_kv-1 redundant global loads per lane eliminated.
optimizations.expectedSpeedup: small (8 fp32 loads * (n_kv-1) per lane removed); meaningful at long context where the QJL score loop dominates the per-step cost.

remaining: P2 — cp.async / TMA double-buffered K-sign/V-block tile staging on sm_80+ / sm_90+: the __shared__ tiling seam P0 added (sh_dec, plus the existing kv_tile arg) is the hook, but a graph-wired harness is needed to benchmark the double-buffer win before flipping it on; deferred.

P3 — promote qjl_score_dp4a_kernel to the default standalone QJL score path: needs fork-side wiring of GGML_OP_ATTN_SCORE_QJL to the int8-sketch kernel + a real-graph round-trip accuracy check (the cuda_verify cross-check shows the int8 round-trip diff is ~1.4e-1 on the fixture magnitudes, which is fine for scores but must be confirmed on a real trace); the kernel is authored, the switch is not flipped.

P3 — occupancy: 1 warp/block at 32 threads under-uses an SM; running 2-4 (head,pos) warps/block sharing the K/V tile in shared memory needs nvcc --ptxas-options=-v + Nsight Compute on the target arch to tune — not done.
Native sm_120 SASS — needs nvcc >= 12.8 (the distro toolkit here is 12.0; compute_90 PTX JIT works for correctness but is not the tuned Blackwell SASS).
